name: California Dietitian Registration
description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to California - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: No State Licensing Board
provider.parentOrganization: Title Protection Only (Business and Professions Code Section 2585)

address.description: 120 South Riverside Plaza, Suite 2000, Chicago, IL 60606-6995

processingTime: 1 to 2 Weeks (for exam authorization after application submission)

requiredDocuments.name: Proof of CDR Registration
requiredDocuments.description: Employers will require proof of your active CDR credential.

mainEntity.name: How much does a California dietitian license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: California does not charge a state licensing fee because the state does not issue dietitian licenses. You only pay your standard national registration and exam fees directly to the CDR.

mainEntity.name: What are the continuing education requirements for a California Dietitian?

acceptedAnswer.text: Because there is no state board, there are no state-mandated CE hours. You simply follow the national CDR requirement of completing 75 CPEUs every five years to maintain your credential.

mainEntity.name: Does California have reciprocity for dietitians?

acceptedAnswer.text: California is currently a CDR State. There is no separate state endorsement transfer pathway right now; maintain an active CDR credential to practice.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to California?

acceptedAnswer.text: This is currently a CDR State, so there is no state transfer timeline. You can usually start once your CDR credential and employer onboarding are complete.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in California while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: There is no active state endorsement application to wait on right now. Start dates are typically driven by employer credentialing and onboarding requirements.
